abstract: Abstract Despite offering substantial opportunities to tailor services to consumers’ wants and needs, artificial intelligence (AI) technologies often come with ethical and operational challenges. One salient instance of such challenges emerges when vulnerable consumers, consumers who temporarily or permanently lack resource access or control, are unknowingly discriminated against, or excluded from the marketplace. By integrating the literature on consumer vulnerability, AI for social good, and the calls for rethinking marketing for a better world, the current work builds a framework on how to leverage AI technologies to detect, better serve, and empower vulnerable consumers. Specifically, our AID framework advocates for designing AI technologies that make services more accessible , optimize customer experiences and journeys interactively , and to dynamically improve consumer decision-making. Adopting a multi-stakeholder perspective, we also discuss the respective implications for researchers, managers, consumers, and public policy makers.

affiliation: The University of Texas at Austin
authors_full.name: Gizem Yalcin Williams

ob.ai_reason: AI服务弱势消费者，核心AI议题
ob.tldr: 本文提出AID框架，利用AI技术检测、服务并赋能资源受限的弱势消费者，强调可访问性、互动体验优化和动态决策提升，探讨对研究者、管理者、消费者和公共政策的多方影响。

title: Deploying artificial intelligence in services to AID vulnerable consumers
title_zh: 在服务中部署人工智能以援助弱势消费者
